channel.title: The Grant Desk - Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Tech & Infrastructure, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Exygy: Senior Product Designer (Bloom, temporary)
item.description:
Organisation: Exygy

Location: Remote, United States

Award: Paid role: $97,750-$132,250 per year for a temporary engagement. No application fee.

Duration: Temporary engagement Q3 2026 to Q1 2027

Deadline: Closed 08 Jun 2026

Exygy is hiring a temporary Senior Product Designer (UX/UI) to work on Bloom, its affordable-housing platform. Remote within the United States. Compensation: $97,750-$132,250 per year. Engagement runs Q3 2026 to Q1 2027. NOTE: this is a job, not a grant or fellowship. Application deadline Monday, 8 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tech Matters: Tech for Good Project Fellow 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Tech Matters

Location: Remote (worldwide; US-based hires also receive health and related insurance)

Award: Paid: $70,000-$80,000 for US-based employees; compensation adjusted for local circumstances outside the US. No application fee.

Duration: Fixed-term fellowship, approximately 6 to 12 months (flexible)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Tech Matters, the nonprofit founded by Jim Fruchterman (MacArthur Fellow, Skoll Awardee), is hiring a Project Fellow to work directly with the CEO on ensuring technology better serves the 90% of humanity typically neglected by the commercial tech industry. Project work spans (1) expanding the Better Deal for Data, a practical data-governance standard for the nonprofit sector, by supporting nonprofits adopting and endorsing the standard; (2) prototyping methods for improving LLM and search-engine answers on critical social problems (initial focus: preventing sexual abuse of children); and (3) helping develop an open course based on Jim's recent book Technology for Good (CC-licensed, global release September 2026). REMOTE; open to candidates around the world (US-based hires get health and related insurance). WHAT THEY LOOK FOR: demonstrated interest and 5+ years' experience in tech-enabled organisations; Bachelor's/Master's in CS, data science, MLIS, MBA, or social sciences; familiarity with SaaS platforms; clear and kind writing; comfortable in an early-stage team. NICE TO HAVE: Wikipedia editing, data-governance expertise, additional languages. APPLY by emailing a resume and a real (not LLM-generated) cover letter to jobs@techmatters.org with subject 'Tech for Good Project Fellow - [Your Name]'. The cover letter should explain why this role and mission resonate with you. NOTE: this is a job/fellowship, not a grant; no specific application deadline listed (rolling).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: GitHub Secure Open Source Fund
item.description:
Organisation: GitHub

Location: Remote (GitHub Sponsors-supported regions)

Award: $10,000 per project via GitHub Sponsors ($6,000 at the kickoff sprint, $2,000 at 6 months, $2,000 at 12 months), plus security training and community. No application fee.

Duration: 12-month engagement

Deadline: Rolling / undated

GitHub's Secure Open Source Fund supports maintainers to improve the security of widely-used open-source software, pairing funding with hands-on security training. ELIGIBILITY: individual maintainers or teams of up to three, aged 18 or older, in a region supported by GitHub Sponsors; applicants cannot work for GitHub or its parent company. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis; apply once to be considered for upcoming 2026 sessions. Apply via the GitHub Secure Open Source Fund page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FLOSS/fund (Zerodha)
item.description:
Organisation: Zerodha (FLOSS/fund)

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: $10,000 to $100,000 per project (approximately $1,000,000 per year total budget). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

FLOSS/fund, backed by Zerodha, provides financial support to free and open-source software projects and maintainers. ELIGIBILITY: individuals and projects worldwide; applicants apply by publishing a funding.json manifest in their public repository as described on the fund's site. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis with no deadline.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sovereign Tech Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Sovereign Tech Agency (Germany)

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: Investment-style funding for open digital base technologies; amounts vary and are often EUR 100,000+. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Sovereign Tech Fund invests in the open digital infrastructure that underpins the modern internet, funding maintenance, security and development of critical open-source base technologies. ELIGIBILITY: individuals, companies and non-profits maintaining or developing critical open-source infrastructure; open worldwide. Applications are accepted on a rolling, year-round basis via the Sovereign Tech Fund page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prototype Fund Class 03 (Open-Source Software)
item.description:
Organisation: Prototype Fund (Open Knowledge Foundation Germany)

Location: Germany / EU (lead must be based in Germany; funded members must reside in the EU)

Award: Up to EUR 95,000 over 6 months, or up to EUR 158,000 over 10 months, plus coaching. No application fee.

Duration: 6 or 10 months

Deadline: 30 Nov 2026

Prototype Fund supports the development of socially relevant open-source software, offering low-threshold funding and coaching to individual developers and small teams. ELIGIBILITY: individual developers or teams of up to four; funded members must reside in the EU and the lead entity must be based in Germany. Application window 1 October to 30 November 2026. Apply via the Prototype Fund application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Interledger Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Interledger Foundation

Location: Remote (worldwide)

Award: USD 72,000 personal stipend plus a USD 20,000 project budget. No application fee.

Duration: Fellowship term (project-based)

Deadline: 02 Jul 2026

Interledger Foundation fellowship funding individuals to advance open, interoperable digital payment infrastructure (the Interledger Protocol and Open Payments) and broaden digital financial access. ELIGIBILITY: individual researchers, developers, open-source contributors, advocates, creators and artists working on digital financial inclusion; open worldwide. Apply via the Interledger fellowship page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: Closed 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wikimedia Conference Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Wikimedia Foundation

Location: Worldwide (subject to legal eligibility by country)

Award: From USD 10,000 per grant (no preset upper cap stated). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based. Processing approximately 3 months from submission to decision, plus 2-3 weeks for finalisation before disbursement.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Wikimedia Foundation grants supporting the organisation of local, regional and thematic conferences that bring Wikimedians together for knowledge sharing, skill development and professional networking. Two rounds per year. Round 1 deadline: 31 August 2026. Round 2 deadline: 1 February 2027. ELIGIBILITY: groups, organisations and Wikimedia affiliates; individuals may apply via a fiscal sponsor.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding
item.description:
Organisation: Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
